The WIC Show
The WIC TV Show, presented in English and Spanish, offers features on nutrition (including cooking, reading nutrition labels, food portion control), physical activity, breastfeeding, lead poison prevention, and more. The WIC Show is produced by the WIC Supplemental Nutrition Program for WIC clients. To view recent shows on YouTube, click on the links below.
English:
https://www.youtube.com/user/TheWICShow?feature=mhee#p/u
Spanish:
https://www.youtube.com/user/ElProgramadeWIC#p/u

CalFresh
is California’s food stamps (SNAP) program. Apply to get up to $204 a month per household member on an EBT card.

– The USDA offers programs that provide children with nutritious school breakfast and lunch at reduced cost or for free. Learn more about the
National School Lunch and Breakfast programs
. Download the

to learn more about Alameda County school food programs in your local district.

Medi-Cal and Dental Services
Medi-Cal
Medi-Cal provides medical, dental, and vision insurance to low-income families in California.
Apply for Medi-Cal
Who Is Eligible for Medi-Cal?
Adults who are parents or caretaker relatives that have any children under 21 years of age living in your home
Children birth through age 20
Undocumented children and young adults can receive coverage for emergency or pregnancy-related services
For full-scope Medi-Cal, citizenship or legal residency is required
Dental Services
Medi-Cal covers dental services that are provided through Denti-Cal. To obtain a listing of Denti-Cal providers, call 1-800-322-6384 or visit the main
Denti-Cal website
.
